text:
Once upon a time in a verdant forest, there lived a wise old owl named Oliver. He had all kinds of eccentric pets: a talking chipmunk named Chip, a flock of colorful hummingbirds that never flew far enough away from his dinner plate, and once, a curious cat who believed it could talk.

One moonlit night, after a rainy day, Oliver sat on a branch with his cat by his side, reading a book about ancient forest spirits.

“Really?” the cat said skeptically. “Didn’t get that from Mom?”

“Read on,” said Oliver. “Actually…”

The cat paused. “…It says here these spirits… they have a talent for—”

“————turning ordinary things into delightful absurdities?” the owl interrupted. “That’s charming.”

The cat laughed. “You don’t believe in spirits?”

“Why wouldn’t I?”

“Well…”

One night, as the full moon cast a luminous glow over the forest, a peculiar
